rules: [P1] Start with patient-scoped searches across relevant resources before broader or paginated record traversal.
[P1] Use domain-specific codes, aliases, and all relevant record sections to identify prior qualifying events.
[P1] If a requested prerequisite, related, or companion result is absent after searching relevant sources, report it explicitly as not found.

[P1] For imaging reorders, search imaging history broadly by study name/synonyms across results/history, identify the most recent completed qualifying study, and match modality/body region/variant exactly (including contrast status and indication) before deciding whether to reorder.
[P1] If required protocol inputs are absent, do not infer or execute protocol-driven treatment action; state clearly that protocol execution is blocked by missing qualifying data.
[P1] Retrieve the most recent date and value for each requested lab or measurement independently; do not assume related tests were collected on the same date.
[P1] Do not place duration-based follow-up or removal orders when the current documented duration has not exceeded the protocol threshold.

[P1] For order-management tasks, match and reconcile orders at the correct clinical scope by intent and equivalent regimen/class (e.g., therapeutic vs prophylactic are not duplicates); search aliases across that scope, place a new order if none exist, keep the single appropriate active order if exactly one exists, and reconcile duplicates by keeping the newest appropriate order and discontinuing older equivalent active ones.
[P1] Verify that treatment-start criteria require the full documented pattern, not a single qualifying value.
[P1] Escalate missing-data conclusions only after exhausting all relevant record sources, including active documentation and order/discontinuation records.
[P1] Verify time-threshold calculations explicitly against the current date instead of relying on rough recency judgments.
[P1] Do not recommend protocol-driven follow-up when the latest relevant value is within the protocol's normal or non-triggering range.
[P1] For requested measurements, search observation sources such as vitals and flowsheets before concluding no data exist.
[P1] When companion data are missing, apply the protocol to the available qualifying result and note the limitation.

[P1] Before applying duration-based management or placing follow-up/removal orders, confirm the target device or procedure through broad documentation sources (not just a single procedure code), determine duration from insertion or first documented presence across the full relevant history, and verify whether it is still active or has removal/discontinuation documentation.

[P1] For time-windowed averages or summaries, use timestamped observations directly, verify the correct measurement type/source mapping, and filter values separately for each requested window before computing results.

[P1] For medication-based recommendations or safety checks, start with focused patient-scoped active medication/order searches and cover all relevant representations, aliases, and classes before concluding presence or absence.
[P1] For repeat-order or interval-based decisions, identify the most recent completed qualifying study/procedure from results/history (not just orders) and use that date.

[P1] For immunization recency and repeat-vaccine decisions, search immunization history and administration records (not just orders or a single code), use the most recent documented administration date, and only order if no recent qualifying administration exists.

[P1] Before creating orders in workflows that may require reconciliation, verify that the available tools support needed update/discontinue actions; if not, avoid creating an uncorrectable duplicate state and report the limitation.
[P1] If no prior qualifying event is documented, treat the patient as due or overdue under the applicable time-based protocol.

[P1] For protocol-driven companion or safety orders, identify the qualifying active trigger treatment set first (including class/alias coverage), and add the companion only if no equivalent active companion order exists.
[P1] Sanity-check time-windowed results: nested or different windows should only match when the underlying observations truly support identical counts or values.
[P1] Complete the requested workflow after retrieval: perform the needed calculations or decision step and report the requested result.
[P1] Double-check units and rounding after calculations or aggregation.
